metadata.title: The Influence of Oxytocin on the Mirror Neuron System: Developing Brain Imaging Paradigms for Future Therapeutic Interventions

annotations.reasoning: Oxytocin is a small peptide hormone consisting of 9 amino acids. However, it is not used as the active drug in this clinical trial. The study investigates its effect on brain activity and social cognition, but the primary intervention is not oxytocin itself being used as a therapeutic agent.

evidence.title: The Influence of Oxytocin on the Mirror Neuron System: Developing Brain Imaging Paradigms for Future Therapeutic Interventions
evidence.snippet: Title: The Influence of Oxytocin on the Mirror Neuron System: Developing Brain Imaging Paradigms for Future Therapeutic Interventions

evidence.title: Brief Summary
evidence.snippet: The purpose of this project is to investigate the possible effect of intranasally administered Oxytocin (OT) on specific mirror neuron areas in human brain. The mirror neuron system (MNS) is thought to be involved in action perception and understanding, and may also underlie more complex cognitive processes such as imitation. We will use electroencephalographic (EEG) investigations to examine brain activity while participants complete two different tasks, consisting of the observation and imitat

evidence.title: Study Status
evidence.snippet: Status: COMPLETED

evidence.title: Conditions & Keywords
evidence.snippet: Conditions: Social Cognition, Expressed Emotion | Keywords: Mirror neurons, Imitation, Oxytocin

evidence.title: Intervention: Oxytocin
evidence.snippet: DRUG: Oxytocin - The experimental oxytocin group will receive 24 international units (IU) of oxytocin using an intranasal administration (self-administered nasal spray) one time before the experimental session

evidence.title: Intervention: Placebo
evidence.snippet: OTHER: Placebo - The placebo comparator group will receive sterile saline through intranasal administration, consisting of the same salt solution in which the hormone will be dissolved , but lacking the hormone itself.

evidence.title: Phase
evidence.snippet: Phase: EARLY_PHASE1

evidence.title: FDA Label: Oxytocin
evidence.snippet: Route: ORAL

Reasoning: Step 1 — The intervention is oxytocin, and Peptide determination = False → STOP → answer "Other". Since the intervention is not a peptide, it cannot be classified as an AMP.

opinions.reasoning: While oxytocin has some structural similarities to peptides, it is classified as a hormone, not a peptide therapeutic.  The trial focuses on the effects of oxytocin on brain activity related to mirror neurons.

The product name "Oxytocin" indicates that it is a biological substance. Although it is administered via oral (intranasal) route in this context, oxytocin is classified as a peptide due to its chemical composition and function as a hormone.
Oxytocin is a non-peptide hormone, it's not a peptide therapeutic as defined by the critical rules.

fields.reconciler_reasoning: Despite verifier_2's argument about chemical composition, the primary evidence from clinicaltrials.gov clearly indicates that oxytocin is being studied as a hormone for its effects on mirror neurons. The trial does not classify or treat oxytocin as a peptide therapeutic. Therefore, based on the provided evidence and context of the study, the conservative interpretation aligns with verifiers 1 and 3 who agree that oxytocin should be classified as False (not a peptide).

opinions.reasoning: Oxytocin, while a peptide, is not an antimicrobial peptide. This trial focuses on its effects on the mirror neuron system and social cognition, not on directly killing or inhibiting pathogens.

The peptide nature of oxytocin is confirmed by the openFDA data indicating that it is administered orally (which includes intranasal administration). However, being a peptide alone does not classify this as an antimicrobial peptide. The core test for AMPs involves direct antimicrobial mechanisms such as killing, inhibiting, or disrupting pathogens, which are not mentioned in the evidence provided about the study's objectives and outcomes. Oxytocin is a neuropeptide, not an antimicrobial peptide. It does not have a direct antimicrobial mechanism or stimulate immune defense against pathogens. Instead, it plays a role in social interactions and reproduction. Therefore, it falls under the 'Other' category according to the decision tree provided.

The evidence provided does not contain any published results that would indicate a failure in meeting the primary endpoint(s) of the study. identificationModule.officialTitle: The Influence of Oxytocin on the Mirror Neuron System: Developing Brain Imaging Paradigms for Future Therapeutic Interventions

responsibleParty.investigatorTitle: Interim Director, Maryland Psychiatric Research Center

descriptionModule.briefSummary: The purpose of this project is to investigate the possible effect of intranasally administered Oxytocin (OT) on specific mirror neuron areas in human brain. The mirror neuron system (MNS) is thought to be involved in action perception and understanding, and may also underlie more complex cognitive processes such as imitation. We will use electroencephalographic (EEG) investigations to examine brain activity while participants complete two different tasks, consisting of the observation and imitation of emotional facial expressions (video presented) and the observation and execution of simple grasping actions (live presented), respectively. In addition, before the beginning of the EEG recording session, subjects will be randomly assigned to two different groups (Oxytocin or Placebo group) and will receive a dose of either intranasal Oxytocin or Placebo solution. Oxytocin is a polypeptide hormone that plays a critical role in social behavior. We will identify mu and beta rhythm from the ongoing EEG and examine suppression as a function of emotion and goal directed action perception and imitation/execution.

armGroups.label: Oxytocin
armGroups.description: The experimental oxytocin group will receive 24 international units (IU) of oxytocin using an intranasal administration (self-administered nasal spray) one time before the experimental session.

armGroups.label: Placebo
armGroups.description: The placebo comparator group will receive sterile saline through intranasal administration, consisting of the same salt solution in which the hormone will be dissolved , but lacking the hormone itself.

interventions.name: Oxytocin
interventions.description: The experimental oxytocin group will receive 24 international units (IU) of oxytocin using an intranasal administration (self-administered nasal spray) one time before the experimental session

interventions.name: Placebo
interventions.description: The placebo comparator group will receive sterile saline through intranasal administration, consisting of the same salt solution in which the hormone will be dissolved , but lacking the hormone itself.

primaryOutcomes.description: The EEG mu rhythm suppression is an index of the human mirror neuron system (alpha and lower beta band activity (8-13 and \~20Hz) recorded over the sensorimotor cortex. EEG will be recorded during the performance of an emotion imitation task (Carr et al., 2003) in which participants will be asked to imitate and internally generate facial emotions seen on a computer screen, or to simply observe, as well as performing a simple grasping action task. Changes in the mu suppression while the performance of these tasks, before and after oxytocin/placebo administration, will be the primary outcome measure.
primaryOutcomes.timeFrame: Before oxytocin/placebo administration and 45 minutes after

eligibilityModule.eligibilityCriteria:
Inclusion Criteria:

* Right-hand dominant
* Sufficient spoken English so as to be able to complete testing validly and give informed consent

Exclusion Criteria:

* Currently taking any psychotropic or cognition enhancing medication
* History of schizophrenia or any other psychotic disorder, including Bipolar Disorder
* Family history of psychosis
* Neurological disease or clinically significant head injury
* Physical disability that substantially impairs motor function
* Current diagnosis of polydypsic-induced hyponatremia
* Substance dependence or abuse in the past 6 months

locations.facility: Child Development Lab, University of Maryland

references.citation: Carr L, Iacoboni M, Dubeau MC, Mazziotta JC, Lenzi GL. Neural mechanisms of empathy in humans: a relay from neural systems for imitation to limbic areas. Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A. 2003 Apr 29;100(9):5497-502. doi: 10.1073/pnas.0935845100. Epub 2003 Apr 7.
